intro: I'm a software engineer focused on architecture, complex systems, and long-term maintainability. I've owned complete implementations on multi-person teams and served as the sole developer on large production projects. My work tends to live where product complexity and technical rigor meet.

principles.title: Predictable over clever
principles.description: I optimize for correctness and stability first. Explicit state, clear data flow, minimal magic. Code should be easy to understand and maintain six months later.

principles.title: Thinking in constraints
principles.description: I start by identifying failure modes and second-order effects. Understanding what can go wrong leads to more durable solutions than assuming everything will work.

principles.title: Clarity over persuasion
principles.description: My teaching background shows up in how I write, explain, and document systems. I care more about being understood than sounding impressive.

principles.title: Tradeoffs, not best practices
principles.description: Every decision has costs. I prefer discussing real tradeoffs rather than defaulting to dogma. Context matters more than convention.

principles.title: Trust the person closest to the problem
principles.description: The operator, the customer, the developer. They know their context better than any system can. I build software that gives people real control and clear information, not interfaces that make decisions for them.

principles.title: Clarity is a form of respect
principles.description: When someone is navigating a complex system under pressure, every moment of confusion is a moment they might lose trust. Clear design isn't a nice-to-have.

principles.title: Close the gap between intent and outcome
principles.description: When someone knows what they want but the tool makes it hard to get there, that's a design failure, not a user failure. I treat that gap as the most important thing to shrink.
